news.headline: India vs Pakistan Conflict Escalates: UN Exposes Civilian Attacks | Defence News | Afghan Pak War
news.news_summary: India vs Pakistan Conflict Escalates: UN Exposes Civilian Attacks | Defence News | Afghan Pak War | India’s stand under the PM Modi government has been vindicated. A UN report has dropped a bombshell on Pakistan, accusing its military of potential war crimes in Afghanistan after deadly air strikes that reportedly killed 400+ civilians, including hospital patients in Kabul. The UN says Pakistan may have violated international law and cannot claim self defence without proof against the Taliban. India had already warned about the dangers of these cross-border terror attacks. Now the world is watching. Will Pakistan face accountability? And how will this impact India Pakistan tensions and regional geopolitics? Watch this Inconnect News defence update for more details! #India #IndiaPakistanWar #IndiaPakistanNews #IndiavsPakistan #IndiaPakistan #OperationSindoor #War #PahalgamAttack #IndianArmy #IndianAirForce #IndianNavy #PMModi #Modi #Kashmir #IndiaPakistanBorder #IndiaPakWar #Geopolitics #news #currentaffairs #worldaffairs #worldnews UN Exposes Pakistan's Civilian Attacks — India vs Pakistan Tensions Explode | A United Nations report accusing Pakistan of potential war crimes in Afghanistan has triggered intense global debate and added a dramatic new dimension to the India Pakistan Conflict narrative. According to UN experts, Pakistani air strikes inside Afghanistan allegedly killed more than 400 civilians, including patients at a hospital in Kabul, raising serious concerns about violations of international law. This development is dominating World News, India News, and Latest News discussions as analysts examine the wider geopolitical implications. The UN report states that Pakistan’s cross-border attacks may violate the United Nations Charter, questioning Islamabad’s claim of self-defence. Experts also say there is no credible evidence that the Afghan authorities directed attacks by militant groups against Pakistan. These findings have intensified debate around the escalating Afghan Pak War, also widely discussed as the Afghanistan Pakistan War or Pak Afghan War. India had earlier condemned Pakistan’s attack on civilian infrastructure, calling it a threat to regional stability. The developments are now being viewed through the lens of the broader India Vs Pakistan Conflict, which continues to dominate India Pakistan News and Defence News coverage. The crisis comes at a time when tensions between the two countries remain high after the Pahalgam Attack in Kashmir, which killed civilians and triggered strong responses from New Delhi. Following that attack, the government led by PM Modi launched Operation Sindoor, during which the Indian Army targeted terror infrastructure across the LOC. These events have further shaped the wider narrative around India Vs Pakistan War scenarios and the long-standing India Pakistan War rivalry that continues to influence regional Defence strategies. This story reflects a significant moment in regional geopolitics and international law. For students preparing for UPSC and those tracking strategic affairs, the unfolding crisis highlights how conflicts between Pakistan and Afghanistan intersect with the larger India Vs Pakistan security environment. Tune in for Inconnect News Insights and the latest news on Defence and Geopolitics. Should the international community investigate Pakistan for war crimes? Will this UN report change global perception of Pakistan? news.headline: Adani Defence And Aerospace Delivers First Batch Of 'Prahar' Guns To Indian Army
news.news_summary:
Adani Defence And Aerospace Delivers First Batch Of 'Prahar' Guns To Indian Army

The Indian Army received the first batch of 2,000 indigenously manufactured 'Prahar' light machine guns (LMGs) from Adani Defence and Aerospace in partnership with Israel Weapon Industries on Saturday. The 7.62 mm-calibre weapons are part of a larger order of over 40,000 guns and have been produced in India under the 'Make in India' programme. With the delivery completed 11 months ahead of schedule, the LMGs were handed over at the company's small arms complex in Gwalior. Additional Secretary and Director General (Acquisition) in the Defence Ministry, A Anbarasu, flagged off trucks carrying the first consignment alongside CEO of Adani Defence and Aerospace, Ashish Rajvanshi. #AdaniDefence #Aerospace #MachineGuns #MakeInIndia #NEGEV #LMG #IsraelWeapon #IndianArmy About NDTV (English news channel): NDTV is India's Most-Trusted News Broadcaster with the latest updates in news, sports, entertainment and much more from within India and around the world. news.headline: Mumbai Indians has the edge, says Vidyut Sivaramakrishnan | The Hindu IPL Podcast 2026
news.news_summary:
Mumbai Indians has the edge, says Vidyut Sivaramakrishnan | The Hindu IPL Podcast 2026

Mumbai Indians has traditionally been the bogey team for Kolkata Knight Riders, particularly at the Wankhede Stadium, where the home side enjoys a commanding 10-2 head-to-head record. But, does KKR have a chance to win, given the last time MI won its season-opener was back in 2012? In this episode of the IPL podcast, The Hindu’s Sports Editor K.C. Vijaya Kumar and Vidyut Sivaramakrishnan decode the Sunday night’s Indian Premier League clash between the former champions.

news.headline: Iran Claims Missiles Targeted U.S. Aircraft Including F-16; Sirens In Israeli Nuclear Town
news.news_summary: Iran said its forces and allied groups carried out a series of strikes on Israeli-linked targets and American aircraft including an F-16 on Saturday. According to Iran’s state-run Press TV, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), alongside the Iranian army and regional allies, used long- and medium-range missile systems as well as attack drones to target what it described as “Israeli-American” industrial sites in Israel and elsewhere. The report also claimed that Iranian forces shot down a U.S. MQ-9 drone and struck an F-16 fighter jet; these claims have not been independently verified.

news.headline: Jets Down, Radars Gone; Billions Wiped Out As Iran War Bleeds U.S. Taxpayers
news.news_summary: According to The Wall Street Journal, the early weeks of the ongoing US-Iran conflict have inflicted billions of dollars in military losses. The report details several incidents, including downed F-15 and F-35 jets, a mid-air collision between KC-135 refuelling aircraft that killed six crew members, and multiple drones destroyed. Iranian strikes have also targeted radar and support systems at bases across Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Jordan. At sea, the USS Gerald R. Ford experienced a fire and is undergoing repairs in Greece. In response, the Pentagon is preparing a $200 billion supplemental spending request to replace damaged systems and maintain operational readiness, according to the report.
